text: Average Peak Occupancy (Tuesday to Thursday) 36%* Americas . Across the Americas, workstation occupancy averages 36% during mid-week peaks, highlighting lower desk use and wide variation between offices. 45%* EMEA . In Europe, the Middle East, and Africa, workstation utilization sits at 45% on core weekdays, with strong performers balanced by sites with far lower use. 48%* APAC . For Asia-Pacific, average workstation occupancy reaches 48% during mid-week peaks, though results vary significantly despite some offices operating at high levels. *Visual representation only. Data accurate as of November 2025. For most recent data, please submit your details to download the latest report . industry expertise Meet the author behind the insights . John Cowx . John Cowx is Head of Business Intelligence at Freespace, with over 20 years of industry experience. His talent for translating complex workplace data into actionable insights is helping today’s leading organizations make informed real estate decisions, optimize space, workplace performance, and employee experience. Connect on: LinkedIn Your questions answered . What is the Freespace IQ Global Workplace Insights Report? The Freespace IQ Global Workplace Insights Report is a quarterly research publication that combines Freespace’s proprietary occupancy and booking data with trusted industry benchmarks. It provides insights into global workplace trends, hybrid work patterns, space utilization, and data-driven design recommendations for real estate and workplace leaders. Who is this report for? The report is designed for corporate real estate leaders, workplace strategists, designers, and anyone interested in understanding how offices are evolving. It helps organizations benchmark their performance, make informed space decisions, and plan workplace strategies based on data. What kind of data is included? The report draws on anonymized insights from over 185,000 managed office spaces and more than 8 million annual bookings . It includes occupancy rates, meeting room and desk usage, behavioral patterns, and trends at city, regional, and global levels, supplemented with industry research for comparison. How often is the report published? The report is released quarterly, giving readers a regular, up-to-date view of evolving workplace trends. Subscribers automatically receive the latest edition directly in their inbox. Can the insights be applied to my own workplace? Yes. While the report is based on aggregate data across Freespace clients and global markets, the insights and recommendations can help organizations evaluate their own space usage, benchmark performance, and guide office design or hybrid work strategies. Please note, this report is not an evaluation of your specific workplace. If you want to understand how your own office is performing, we recommend conducting a dedicated analysis – our free occupancy trial is a good place to start, with no commitment required. How do I access the report? You can get your free copy by filling out the form on this page. Once submitted, you’ll receive a link to download the PDF, and future editions will be delivered automatically to your inbox each quarter.
meta_description: Discover the global office trends shaping how and where people work. This report combines data from 185,000+ spaces and 8 million bookings.

text: Is the trial free? All equipment, software, and reporting are included at no cost. You only cover a small one-time setup fee for installation. What happens after the trial? You keep all insights and reports, with no obligation to continue. If you choose to expand, scaling is quick and seamless. What kind of spaces can be measured? Desks, meeting rooms, phone booths, collaboration or open areas, providing a full picture of your workplace utilization, occupancy and behavioural patterns. How long does the trial last? The trial runs for 3 months and provides full visibility into how your workplace is used, including desk and meeting room occupancy and overall space  utilization. How long does set-up take? Most trials are up and running in under two weeks, with professional installation and no IT hurdles. Do I need any IT or technical expertise? No. The trial includes all hardware, software, and network setup. Our team handles installation, configuration, and training – so you can focus on the insights, not technology.
